title: SWEC iEEG Dataset
description: A large-scale intracranial EEG dataset comprising 5376 hours of continuous long-term monitoring recordings from 40 patients with pharmacoresistant epilepsy, acquired at the Sleep-Wake-Epilepsy-Center (SWEC), University of Bern. The dataset includes 381 annotated electrographic seizures with precise onset/offset annotations by board-certified epileptologists. Data were recorded using intracranial strip, grid, and depth electrodes at sampling rates of 512 or 1024 Hz and preprocessed with digital band-pass filtering (0.5–150 Hz). The fully anonymized public release is formatted in BIDS-compliant BrainVision format.
methods_description: Intracranial EEG recordings were acquired using strip, grid, and depth electrodes with 16-bit analog-to-digital conversion at sampling rates of 512 or 1024 Hz per subject. Data were digitally band-pass filtered (0.5–150 Hz, 4th-order Butterworth, zero-phase) and referenced to the median across channels. Artifact-contaminated channels were removed at the source. Seizure onsets and offsets were annotated by board-certified epileptologist Prof. Kaspar Schindler.
